title: Passive Microwave Radiometry
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is widefield-fluorescence template (fan-out artifact). Physics below is grounded in canonical remote-sensing literature for this principle.

E.description: Passive Microwave Radiometry: microwave radiometry produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG L.radiometer -> L.antenna_pattern -> int.temporal, with time-integrated exposure and additive Gaussian thermal/electronic noise. Recovery is posed as a linear inverse problem that inverts the forward operator to estimate the scene-side 2D brightness temperature. Difficulty tier delta=3 with effective condition number kappa_eff~7; calibration-level mismatch (antenna_sidelobe, land_contamination, RFI) sets the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form imaging equation.
E.forward_model: T_b(theta, phi) = integral antenna_pattern(theta, phi) * T_physical(surface) * emissivity + atm_contrib + n
E.world_state_x: Scene-side quantity of interest (reflectance / velocity / phase / concentration)
E.observation_y: Sensor output at declared resolution + noise

W.regime: Existence of the recovered 2D brightness temperature is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by the declared priors. Stability is well-conditioned (kappa_eff ~= 7); antenna_sidelobe dominates the stability cliff; land_contamination and the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Additive gaussian thermal/electronic noise sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor, while mild Tikhonov or analytic inversion is sufficient at the nominal Omega point.

C.solver_class: linear-operator + convex optimisation [GMI-Backus-Gilbert, Brightness-Interp] | linear-operator + deep neural prior [MW-UNet]
C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: ||x_hat - x||_2 <= C1 * sigma_noise / sqrt(N_measurements). Mismatch-induced bias adds + C2 * |antenna_sidelobe| + C3 * ||x - x_s||_1 (best s-sparse / low-rank approximation residual). Iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the MLE; epsilon_fn captures the monotone quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(H * W * log(...)) per iteration; learned variants: O(H W Z * F_theta_cost) per forward pass
